questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.His <u>industrious</u> attitude was the reason for his promotion.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Terminate
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Peace
Which of the following sentences contains the ANTONYM of the word ‘important’?
questions.option_1: Choose the right colour to make your bike stand out!
questions.option_2: Namita also makes some very salient points in her newly revised preface.
questions.option_3: I have some urgent questions for you.
questions.option_4: You always make me do all the trivial stuff.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the sentence.She was asked by the elders to wear a skirt <u>covering</u> her knees.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word Abundance
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.The army <u>advanced</u> into enemy territory.

Read the given sentence carefully. Change the meaning of the sentence by replacing the underlined word with its ANTONYM from the following options.William Shakespeare is considered to be the most <u>celebrated</u> dramatist in the world of literature.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The company has to be able to <u>compete</u> globally.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the bracketed word in the following sentence to fill in the blank.Lewis was aggressive, ________ (normal), moody, and brilliantly clever.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The dog was <u>agitated</u> and bit the child.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The coach was <u>optimistic</u> that Ronald will play the quarter final.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.The poet was lost in a <u>reverie</u>.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.The encounter made the audience <u>squirm</u>.
Fill in the blank with the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.In spring the leaves are <u>bright</u> green, whereas they appear ______ in autumn.
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.Harry’s behaviour was very <u>blunt</u> during his school days.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.We should not run after <u>transient</u> pleasures of the world.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Timid
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Sluggish
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Enormous
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.The government is planning to <u>abolish</u> child care leave for female employees.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Powerful
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM for the underlined word in the given sentence.Because the politician made a <u>candid</u> speech, he earned the respect of the voters.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Innovate
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Calm

Read the given sentence carefully. Change the meaning of the sentence by replacing the underlined word with its ANTONYM from the following options.His father warned him not to use <u>vulgar</u> language at home.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The stone was also said to have <u>occult</u> powers.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Stoic
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.His <u>feeble</u> opponent was no match to him and ultimately he won the challenge.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank.You can’t stop the _________ (outflow) of current.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Impulsive
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘Commence’ from the given sentence.Let’s put an end to this discussion about voodoo and instead talk about something amusing.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Massive
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.He is not <u>friendly</u> with him in the office.
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.He worked alone as he did not want anyone to <u>hamper</u> his project.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Abandon
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Precarious
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The vendor said that the apples were of <u>superior</u> quality.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Queer
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.I left the job because of a <u>hostile</u> working environment.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘Contaminate’ from the given sentence.Crews were requested to purify the system since a brain-eating amoeba was discovered in the drinking water.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The athlete looked <u>unsettled</u> before the race.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM for the given word.Ghastly
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.The waters of Germany are <u>abundant</u> in fish, but there are few genera and species in them.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Compulsory
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.I have left it to his <u>discretion</u> to accept or decline my offer.
